entries.rationale: KTX conversions are non-urgent interoperability cleanup and will be removed in focused repository and migration-boundary changes.
The production launcher icon requires reviewed original artwork and adaptive-icon assets rather than a placeholder generated by remediation.
Existing boundary parsers and feature state branches are decomposed by the Wave 3 and Wave 4 ownership and state packages.
Existing feature orchestrators are reduced by the Wave 4 state-decomposition and module-boundary packages.
The existing declaration remains readable until its owning repository boundary is decomposed.
Large Food and Training classes are explicit Wave 4 state-decomposition targets and cannot be split safely ahead of their behavior coverage.
Legacy Compose and ViewModel methods are reduced incrementally by the planned feature-state and UI-boundary packages.
Existing stateless Compose callback surfaces are replaced only after restoration and state-holder contracts are merged.
The existing parser loop is retained until its focused boundary package can preserve malformed-input behavior with tests.
Existing chart tokens remain properties until the shared design-system extraction owns their public shape.
Guard-heavy parsers and feature reducers are simplified with their Wave 3 boundary and Wave 4 state packages.
The existing Food fallback converts a failed optional operation into UI state; typed result modeling will make that policy explicit.
Validation branches move to typed boundary results in the Wave 3 error-modeling packages.
Archive, network, and repository boundaries retain broad rollback catches until their typed error packages land.
Feature ViewModels and repositories are decomposed in the ordered ownership, state, and module packages.
The parameters belong to incomplete OCR and routine-editor callback contracts and remain until their owning feature packages complete them.
The stale plate-list parser is removed with the focused Training state package to avoid colliding with that ViewModel family.
Existing explicit IllegalStateException messages are part of user-facing boundary translation and are not mechanically rewritten.
Endpoint validation exception shape remains stable until the Wave 3 network result-modeling package owns it.
